sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI ITR 228 of 2005 ITAs 260 to 262 of 2003 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For hearing of main case. 06.11.2025 Mr. Nawaz Mirza, advocate holding brief for Mr. Shaiq Usmani, advocate for the applicant. Mr. Faheem Ali Memon, advocate for the respondent. Counsel for the respondent states that questions framed for determination inter alia including enforcement of fundamental rights could not be enforced in reference jurisdiction. Be that as it may, these reference applications are pending since 2003 without any progress and even today brief is being held on behalf of applicant’s counsel and adjournment is being sought for no cogent reason. It appears that the applicants are not interested in the present proceedings. Accordingly, in view of judgment of CIR vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, these reference applications are dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this order may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Office is instructed to place copy hereof in the connected files. Judge Judge Khuhro/PS
